texto: I felt the full breadth and depth of the ocean around the sphere of the Earth, back billions of years to the beginning of life, across all the passing lives and deaths, the endless waves of swimming joy and quiet losses of exquisite creatures with fins and fronds, tentacles and wings, colourful and transparent, tiny and huge, coming and going. There is nothing the ocean has not seen
explicacao: Citação de Sally Andrew sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “I felt the full breadth and depth of the ocean around the sphere of the Earth, back billions of y…” articula vida, nature, ocean em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como luto e termino.

texto: Under Capitalism the most important thing is to squeeze out more and more money for a few people to spend. This means that most of the other people -- as well as the earth and the sea and all the creatures and plants and goggas -- have to pay the price.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Sally Andrew sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “Under Capitalism the most important thing is to squeeze out more and more money for a few people …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, politics, people, útil em momentos de termino e reflexao.

texto: Life (like love) is forgiving and hopeful and, like a bold flower pushing out from the narrow crevice in a rock, will take any gap we allow. However small or large.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Sally Andrew: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Life (like love) is forgiving and hopeful and, like a bold flower pushing out from the narrow cre…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, nature, forgiving pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de nature.
